detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent is Microsoftâs acquisition of Activision Blizzard a successful growth strategy within the video gaming industry?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Explicit and consistent use of the key concept âchangeâ throughout the investigation
Clear connection of the concept to Microsoftâs strategic shifts in portfolio and industry context
weaknesses: Definition of âchangeâ in the introduction is broad and lacks citation
Integration, while strong, occasionally feels mechanical rather than fully woven into every argument
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place this work firmly in band 4: the concept of change is used analytically and linked to Microsoftâs context, but it is not yet seamless or grounded in theoretical citation as required for the top band. A score of 4/5 accurately reflects strong understanding and integration.

strengths: Appropriate number of documents (four) fitting the 3â5 range
Supporting documents (annual and industry reports) are relevant to market-share and ratio calculations
weaknesses: Inclusion of a YouTube video reduces academic rigor
Depth of triangulation is limited, with singleâyear snapshots and missing inâtable source notes
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: With four relevant documents but limited depth and one nonâacademic source, this submission sits in band 3. The evidence is sufficient but lacks the breadth and inâdepth justification required for band 4.

strengths: Selection of Ansoff Matrix, market-share metric and profitability ratios aligns with growth-strategy evaluation
Each tool is applied to Microsoftâs post-acquisition scenario
weaknesses: Range of frameworks is narrow (no Porterâs Five Forces, PESTLE, etc.)
Applications are at times descriptive rather than deeply analytical or benchmarked
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the chosen tools are relevant, the selection is not sufficiently broad or sophisticated and applications lack depth. This merits a score of 2/4, reflecting competent but limited use of business theories and tools.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Use of varied data (market share, NPM, cloud-growth) enables interpretation
Analysis moves beyond description, weighing advantages/limitations and reflecting on short- versus long-term impacts
weaknesses: Some data are incomplete or incorrectly sourced (formula errors, missing growth percentages)
Evaluative comments occasionally lack quantitative support and deeper benchmarking
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Most analysis is effective, with sustained integration and critical reflection, placing this work in the upper mid-band (band 4). A score of 4/5 appropriately rewards thorough analysis while acknowledging areas needing deeper data rigor.

strengths: Conclusions are consistent with the body of evidence presented
The research question is directly addressed and answered
weaknesses: Over-generalised claims (e.g., âexpanding exponentiallyâ) lack empirical backing
Lacks new data or qualifiers to substantiate the degree of success in the final judgement
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Conclusions are coherent and address the research question but remain broad and insufficiently detailed, aligning with band 2. A score of 2/3 reflects this partial fulfilment.

strengths: Logical and standard IB IA structure with clear headings and sub-headings
Comprehensive table of contents enhances navigation
weaknesses: Research question could be placed earlier or highlighted more effectively
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The reportâs organization fully meets the expected structure, meriting the top band (2/2). Structure is appropriate and coherent throughout.

strengths: All required presentation elements are present (title page, figures, bibliography, word count)
Figures and tables are well-labelled and formatted
weaknesses: Inconsistent referencing styles and formatting of formulas
Decimal places and inline syntax vary, affecting readability
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Despite stylistic inconsistencies, all mandatory presentation elements are included and effectively executed. This aligns with band 2, warranting the full 2/2.

strandSelections.reasoning: The IA follows standard IB structure: title page, contents, introduction, analysis, conclusion, bibliography.  Headings and sub-headings provide logical flow; no major organisational flaws are evident, satisfying the âappropriate structureâ criterion.

The annual report, industry reports and statistics directly inform the market-share table, market-growth graph and ratio calculations, indicating relevance.  Depth is acceptable but not extensive; several data points are single-year snapshots and there is limited triangulation, so the evidence is âsufficientâ rather than wide-ranging or richly detailed.

All stipulated presentation elements are present: title page with word count, table of contents, labelled figures, bibliography and academic honesty statement.  There is no indication of any missing mandatory component, so the highest presentation band is justified.

Four supporting documents are cited, fitting squarely within the 3â5 range required.  The student did not exceed the limit nor fall short, so the mid-band that simply confirms satisfactory quantity is appropriate.

Each tool is applied to Microsoftâs situation, and links to the research question are generally made (e.g., Ansoff showing diversification, ratios reflecting post-acquisition profitability).  Nonetheless, the applications are sometimes descriptive and lack depthâfor instance, the market-share percentage change is presented without benchmarking significanceâso relevance is clear but effectiveness is uneven.

The student gathers market-share figures, NPM data and cloud-growth statistics that allow some comparison over two years; this constitutes an adequate selection and basic use.  Data are not always fully sourced or explained, and some numbers (e.g., formula placeholders) appear incomplete, showing that effectiveness is moderate rather than strong.

Most sections move beyond description to interpret what the data imply for Microsoftâs strategy, weighing advantages and limitations and occasionally acknowledging short-run versus long-run impacts.  Integration across tools is apparent, though uneven, and some evaluative comments lack depth, placing the work in the âmostly effective analysis with some integrationâ band.

The research question is directly answered (âthe acquisition isâ¦a successful growth strategyâ), but the extent is qualified only briefly and without a clear final judgement of degree.  Hence the response is more than superficial yet not as explicit or detailed as required for the top band.

The student selects the Ansoff Matrix, market-share metric and profitability ratiosârecognised HL tools that align with growth-strategy evaluationâshowing an adequate spread.  More advanced HL tools (e.g., Porterâs Five Forces, C/S analysis) are absent, so selection is competent but not notably wide or sophisticated.

The conclusion restates key findings and is generally aligned with preceding evidence, but referencing is broad and no fresh data are marshalled to substantiate final claims.  Occasional over-generalisations (e.g., âcontinue expanding exponentiallyâ) weaken full consistency, so the strand sits at a partial rather than complete match.

All discussion centres on Microsoft and Activision-Blizzard; facts, figures and examples (game franchises, Game Pass implications, market-share data) are regularly linked back to the organisationâs strategic aims.  The analysis shows insight into Microsoftâs competitive context and operating structure, indicating strong connection.  However, a small portion of the essay drifts into generic statements about diversification without explicit Microsoft reference, preventing a âcomprehensive and fully integratedâ judgement.

The student uses the key concept of change explicitly from the introduction onward and revisits it in every analytical section (Ansoff, market share, market growth and ratios).  Although the word is sometimes inserted rather mechanically, the discussion does link change to shifts in portfolio, hierarchy and industry dynamics, demonstrating a clear and largely accurate understanding.  Integration is visible in several paragraphs but not woven seamlessly into every argument, so the work falls short of the âfully integrated throughoutâ expectation for the top band, but clearly exceeds simple or occasional application.

annotations.comment: Clarify the degree to which the acquisition answers the research question (e.g., percent success or riskâadjusted ROI) to make your conclusion explicit.
The acknowledgment of the acquisitionâs shortâterm perspective demonstrates critical reflection on data limitations.
A YouTube video is cited as a supporting document; replace nonâacademic sources with peerâreviewed or industryâstandard reports.
References exhibit multiple styles; adopt a consistent academic format (e.g., APA or MLA) for all bibliography entries.
The student clearly states âKey Concept: Changeâ on the title page, aligning the investigation with the prescribed IB conceptual framework.
The table of contents is comprehensive and clearly structured, facilitating easy navigation through sections, subsections, figures, and tables.
The definition of âchangeâ in the introduction is broad and lacks citation or theoretical grounding; consider referencing an authoritative source to deepen conceptual rigor.
Reposition the research question earlier in the introduction to anchor the reader immediately; consider highlighting it in its own paragraph or call-out box.
The inline formula syntax âplatform_{(Supporting Document 2)}â is confusing; simplify or remove LaTeX tags in narrative to improve readability.
The phrase âchanging customer baseâ is awkward and unclear; refine this to âexpanding customer segmentsâ for precision in business terminology.
Analysis of advantages and disadvantages is descriptive; strengthen evaluation by citing quantitative evidence (e.g., revenue or cost data) to support each point.
The critique of the Ansoff Matrix is limited to oversimplification; consider integrating an additional framework (e.g., PESTLE) to account for external factors.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The discussion of diversification could reference Figure 2 more clearly; include a direct figure citation in the text (e.g., âsee Figure 2â).
The formula displayed as âMarket Share (%) = \frac{1}{2}â is incorrect; it should be $$\text{Market Share} = \frac{\text{Company Sales}}{\text{Total Market Sales}}\times100$$.
The market share table is well formatted and clearly compares major competitors across two years.
Add an in-table source note or caption under the market share table to clearly reference the supporting document.
Market share percentages use inconsistent decimal places (e.g., 6.4% vs 7.82%); standardize significant figures for clarity.
The market growth section quotes industry expansion but omits specific growth percentages; reference exact figures from Figure 4.
Figure 4 is clearly labelled and effectively visualizes cloud gamingâs annual growth, supporting the discussion.
Embed key data points (e.g., 2023 growth % increase) from Figure 4 directly into your narrative to strengthen evaluative insight.
The net profit margin formula is correctly formatted in LaTeX, enhancing precision of financial analysis.
Include a data source reference directly beneath the financial ratios table to improve traceability.
The discussion of NPM effects is descriptive; deepen analysis by comparing margin trends across competitors and industry benchmarks.
The conclusionâs claim of âexpanding exponentiallyâ lacks new empirical support; temper with evidence or qualifiers to maintain consistency.
The methodology states use of secondary data but does not detail criteria for source selection or evaluation; expand justification to strengthen credibility.
